questions.q: Anurag Kashyap is best known for working primarily in which Indian film industry?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap is a Hindi film director central to India's independent and new-wave cinema.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap's two-part 2012 crime epic about the coal mafia of Dhanbad is titled what?
questions.explanation: Gangs of Wasseypur (2012) is Anurag Kashyap's two-part crime epic set around the Dhanbad coal mafia.

questions.q: In which year was Anurag Kashyap born?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap was born in 1972.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap's 2009 film Dev.D is a modern reworking of which classic Indian novel?
questions.explanation: Dev.D (2009) is Anurag Kashyap's contemporary reinterpretation of Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay's Devdas.

questions.q: Which 2004 Anurag Kashyap film dramatizes the 1993 Bombay bombings?
questions.explanation: Black Friday (2004) is Anurag Kashyap's film dramatizing the 1993 Bombay serial bombings.

questions.q: Black Friday, directed by Anurag Kashyap, was based on a book by which journalist-author?
questions.explanation: Black Friday was adapted from S. Hussain Zaidi's book on the 1993 Bombay bombings.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap co-founded which production company associated with the Indian indie wave?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap co-founded Phantom Films, a hub of the Indian independent movement.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap is primarily celebrated for which style of Hindi storytelling?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap is known for bringing gritty, realist Hindi storytelling to international festivals.

questions.q: Which actor became closely associated with Anurag Kashyap after starring in both parts of Gangs of Wasseypur?
questions.explanation: Nawazuddin Siddiqui rose to prominence through his roles in Anurag Kashyap's Gangs of Wasseypur.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap is best described professionally as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap is a film director central to India's independent and new-wave cinema.

questions.q: At which international film festival did Anurag Kashyap's Gangs of Wasseypur premiere in 2012?
questions.explanation: Gangs of Wasseypur premiered at the 2012 Cannes Directors' Fortnight.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap co-wrote the screenplay for which acclaimed 1998 Ram Gopal Varma crime film?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap co-wrote the screenplay for Ram Gopal Varma's Satya (1998).

questions.q: In Dev.D (2009), which actor played the title role of Dev opposite Mahie Gill?
questions.explanation: Abhay Deol played the title character Dev in Anurag Kashyap's Dev.D.

questions.q: Which actor played the lead role in Anurag Kashyap's surreal 2007 psychological film No Smoking?
questions.explanation: John Abraham starred as the protagonist 'K' in Anurag Kashyap's 2007 surreal film No Smoking.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap's 2009 political film Gulaal, set in the world of student politics in Rajasthan, featured music by which composer?
questions.explanation: Gulaal featured songs written and composed by Piyush Mishra, who also acted in the film.

questions.q: Which music director, known for the song 'Womaniya', composed much of the soundtrack for Anurag Kashyap's Gangs of Wasseypur?
questions.explanation: Sneha Khanwalkar composed much of the Gangs of Wasseypur soundtrack, including 'Womaniya'.

questions.q: Which actress played the matriarch Nagma Khatun in Anurag Kashyap's Gangs of Wasseypur?
questions.explanation: Richa Chadha played Nagma Khatun, the matriarch of the Khan family, in Anurag Kashyap's Gangs of Wasseypur.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap directed which 2018 film about an amateur boxer struggling against a corrupt sporting establishment?
questions.explanation: Mukkabaaz (2018), starring Vineet Kumar Singh, is Anurag Kashyap's boxing drama.

questions.q: Which 2015 period drama set in 1960s Bombay did Anurag Kashyap direct with Ranbir Kapoor and Anushka Sharma?
questions.explanation: Bombay Velvet (2015), starring Ranbir Kapoor and Anushka Sharma, was directed by Anurag Kashyap.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap was a co-director of which influential 2018 Netflix Indian original web series about a Mumbai police investigation?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap co-directed Sacred Games, Netflix's first Indian original series, released in 2018.

questions.q: Sacred Games, co-directed by Anurag Kashyap, was adapted from a novel by which author?
questions.explanation: Sacred Games was adapted from Vikram Chandra's 2006 novel of the same name.

questions.q: Which gritty 2013 Anurag Kashyap thriller, whose title means 'ugly', explores the disappearance of a child?
questions.explanation: Ugly (2013) is Anurag Kashyap's thriller centered on the disappearance of a young girl.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap's directorial debut feature Paanch, completed around 2003, was never theatrically released chiefly because of issues with which body?
questions.explanation: Paanch remained unreleased largely due to certification problems with the Central Board of Film Certification.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap was born in which state of India?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap was born in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, in 1972.

questions.q: Which 2016 Anurag Kashyap film, a reimagining of a notorious serial-killer case, starred Nawazuddin Siddiqui and Vicky Kaushal?
questions.explanation: Raman Raghav 2.0 (2016) reimagined the serial-killer story with Nawazuddin Siddiqui and Vicky Kaushal.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap's 2010 English-language film starring Kalki Koechlin about a woman searching for her father in Mumbai was titled what?
questions.explanation: That Girl in Yellow Boots (2010), co-written with and starring Kalki Koechlin, was directed by Anurag Kashyap.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap was a co-presenter of which 2013 film that premiered at Cannes and starred Irrfan Khan in a story of pen-pals connected by a mistaken lunchbox delivery?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap co-presented The Lunchbox (2013), starring Irrfan Khan, which premiered at the Cannes Film Festival.

questions.q: Which 2015 film backed by Anurag Kashyap, directed by Neeraj Ghaywan and set in Varanasi, won two awards at the Cannes Un Certain Regard section?
questions.explanation: Masaan (2015), produced under Kashyap's banner, won two prizes in the Un Certain Regard section at Cannes.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap directed which 2018 romantic drama featuring Abhishek Bachchan, Taapsee Pannu and Vicky Kaushal?
questions.explanation: Manmarziyaan (2018), with Abhishek Bachchan, Taapsee Pannu and Vicky Kaushal, was directed by Anurag Kashyap.

questions.q: Anurag Kashyap directed one segment of which 2013 anthology film made to mark 100 years of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Anurag Kashyap directed a segment of Bombay Talkies (2013), the anthology celebrating a century of Indian cinema.
